text:
Once upon a time, there was a wise old owl named Willow Whisperer. She lived high in a gnarled oak tree. Every morning, she'd call out to the dawn chorus.

One day, Willow heard some mysterious whispers. They weren't normal whispers; they had lyrics. "Oh no," she thought, "some woodland creatures must be singing me this tune!"

Willow flew down to investigate. There were a group of tiny hummingbirds, chirping something like this:

"Hush little mouse,
Don't speak too loud."

Willow listened and learned their song. The next day, she repeated it back. "Ho-ho, little rodent," they said. "Just quiet for a moment."

She discovered their secret nest tucked into the feathers of a mourning dove. The little birds loved to sing all day. Willow decided to become their guardian.

Each time she sang back, they'd teach her more. Their melody grew more complex—like a symphony.
